exemplar.title: How does Marjane Satrapi utilize a childâs perspective in the graphic novel Persepolis to emphasize identity struggles under political repression during the Islamic Revolution?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Marjane Satrapi utilize a childâs perspective in the graphic novel Persepolis to emphasize identity struggles under political repression during the Islamic Revolution?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Demonstrates good understanding of Persepolis, linking Marjiâs childhood viewpoint to themes of identity, repression and loss of innocence.
Discussion of key episodes (the veil, tortures, Anooshâs arrest, clothing police) shows sustained interpretation of text.
Uses relevant quotations with page references to support central ideas.
weaknesses: Interpretation remains largely explanatory rather than fully persuasive or nuanced.
Range of textual references is somewhat narrow; some episodes are paraphrased rather than directly quoted.
No engagement with counter-readings or secondary sources to deepen interpretation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows good knowledge and understanding by consistently relating major narrative moments to central themes. References are generally relevant and support the argument effectively, though the range is limited. Overall performance meets the descriptors for band 4âsolid understanding with sustained interpretation but not yet at the persuasive excellence of band 5.

strengths: Moves beyond plot summary to analyse Satrapiâs visual choices and symbolism (e.g. black panels, irony of the teacher).
Demonstrates awareness of multimodal elements, such as irony and the bicycle metaphor.
Provides some insightful comments on how specific visuals shape reader response.
weaknesses: Analysis often remains at a descriptive level, stopping at the obvious rather than probing deeper implications.
Multimodal evaluation (panel layout, gutter, iconography) is only superficially addressed.
Evaluation of effectiveness is intermittent; insights are not consistently sustained.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay delivers generally appropriate analysis of textual features and authorial choices, aligning with band 3. While there are moments of insight, discussion is often descriptive and lacks sustained, convincing evaluation required for higher bands.

strengths: Clear line of inquiry articulated in title and introduction, with logically sequenced thematic paragraphs.
Effective topic sentences and transitions that sustain cohesion.
Conclusion neatly summarizes and returns to the thesis, reinforcing structure.
weaknesses: Some repetition of ideas (e.g. multiple references to âloss of innocenceâ) and occasional digressions into background material.
Integration of supporting examples is uneven; long quotations sometimes lack analytical framing.
Inconsistent application of MLA mechanics and formatting detracts from professional presentation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay is adequately organized with some development of a line of inquiry (band 3 for organization) and supporting examples are sometimes integrated into the prose (band 3 for integration). Overall performance fits band 3 for Focus, Organization and Development.

strengths: Language is generally fluent and accurate with varied sentence structures and appropriate academic vocabulary.
Maintains an academic register with few contractions and consistent critical terminology.
Scholarly tone reinforced by clear section headers and a Works Cited list.
weaknesses: Occasional grammatical lapses (agreement, punctuation) and minor spelling errors (âMariiâ).
Some informal phrasings and conversational openings slightly undermine stylistic precision.
Inconsistent citation formatting and incomplete MLA details in the Works Cited.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essayâs language is clear and mostly accurate, demonstrating good control of grammar and vocabulary. Register and style are consistently appropriate to the task, placing the work solidly in band 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Strengths:** Quotations are embedded within explanatory sentences and accompanied by parenthetical page numbers, indicating some integration. Visual details are described within the prose rather than listed separately.  
**Limitations:** Integration is uneven; several passages insert long quotations or panel summaries without analytical framing, and MLA mechanics (ellipses, block quotes) are inconsistently applied. This âsometimesâ coherent embedding meets the descriptor for band 3.

**Strengths:** The student demonstrates a solid understanding of the central concerns of Persepolis, repeatedly linking Marjiâs childhood viewpoint to themes of identity, repression, and loss of innocence. Discussion of key episodesâthe veil, torture stories, Anooshâs arrest, and clothing policeâshows awareness of how personal experience reflects wider sociopolitical forces.  
**Limitations:** Interpretation remains largely explanatory rather than fully persuasive. Nuanced aspects of Satrapiâs narrative voice, historical layering, or metafictional stance are touched on only briefly, and counter-readings are absent. Consequently the response reaches a âgoodâ but not âexcellentâ level of insight, fitting band 4.

**Strengths:** The essay follows a clear line of inquiry stated in the title and introduction, proceeds through logically sequenced thematic paragraphs, and returns to the thesis in a concise conclusion. Topic sentences and transitions generally sustain cohesion.  
**Limitations:** Some repetition (multiple paragraphs reiterate âloss of innocenceâ) and occasional digressions into historical background weaken overall tightness, so organisation is âmostlyâ rather than perfectly cohesive. These traits correspond to band 4.

**Strengths:** Relevant quotations are supplied for every major point (e.g. âToday my name is Che Guevaraâ¦â, âThe revolution is like a bicycleâ¦â, the veil anecdote, Anooshâs arrest). Page references are usually included and the textual moments clearly relate to the argument.  
**Limitations:** The range of references is narrow for a 1 500-word HL essay: many paragraphs rely on extended paraphrase rather than varied, precisely chosen panels or captions. Some references (e.g. clothing episode, torture descriptions) are described but not directly quoted. Support therefore âmostlyâârather than âconsistentlyââbuttresses ideas, placing the work in band 3.

**Strengths:** The essay maintains an academic register, avoiding contractions and personal anecdotes, and consistently employs appropriate critical vocabulary. Citations and a Works Cited list reinforce a scholarly tone.  
**Limitations:** A few conversational openings (âNavigating the world can be difficult for anyoneâ) and imprecise transitions modestly detract from complete stylistic control, situating the work comfortably in band 4 rather than band 5.

**Strengths:** The language is generally fluent and accurate, with varied sentence structures and academic vocabulary (âmultimodal narrativeâ, âjuxtapositionâ, âoverexposureâ). Errors (âMariiâ, âgraphic knowledgeâ) are minor and do not impede comprehension.  
**Limitations:** Occasional lapses in agreement, punctuation, or informal phrasing keep the prose from the precision expected for the highest band. Thus clarity and grammatical control fit band 4.

**Strengths:** The student moves beyond plot summary, analysing Satrapiâs juxtaposition of playful visuals with sombre black panels, symbolism of the veil, and use of irony in the changing teacherâs stance. These moments show awareness of textual features and authorial choices.  
**Limitations:** Analysis often stops at an obvious level (âsolid black background represents oppressionâ) and drifts into general commentary. Graphic-novel techniques such as gutter, layout, and iconography are mentioned only superficially. Evaluation of effectiveness is brief, so insight is intermittent rather than sustained. This aligns with band 3: âgenerally appropriate analysis and evaluationâ.

annotations.comment: The term âgraphic knowledgeâ is vague; consider using precise terminology like âiconographyâ or âvisual rhetoric.â
Insightful note on the teacherâs irony demonstrates engagement with authorial choice and theme of censorship.
Introduce a linking phrase between the Anoosh paragraph and the teacher example to improve coherence.
The question-based title clearly establishes a focused line of inquiry on child perspective and identity, orienting the reader effectively.
Consider converting the question in the title into a concise declarative thesis statement to foreground your argument from the start.
The introductory paragraph begins with a broad generalization; tighten the hook by directly referencing Marjiâs perspective in Persepolis to build immediate relevance.
Replace lengthy historical context with a concise overview tied explicitly to Marjiâs identity struggle, weaving background into your argument more seamlessly.
The quotation about Che Guevara is relevant but followed by paraphrase; deepen analysis by explaining how adopting revolutionary personas shapes Marjiâs identity formation.
Enhance multimodal evaluation by briefly describing panel compositionâframe size, character placement, and speechâbubble designâin your analysis.
The mention of the bicycle metaphor shows awareness of Satrapiâs technique, demonstrating a solid integration of text and image.
Analysis here remains at the descriptive level; explore how gutter spacing and panel sequencing affect reader interpretation and pacing.
The brief introduction to the veil symbol needs stronger contextual depth; engage with a secondary source or cultural analysis to enrich interpretation.
The veil example effectively illustrates childhood resistance; linking Marjiâs games to thematic tension strengthens your argument.
Your parenthetical citation â(3)â does not align with MLA Works Cited entries; verify consistency in your in-text citation format.
Description of Marjiâs facial expression in the torture scene engages with emotional impact effectively, showing good visual analysis.
Consider discussing how the stark blackâandâwhite contrasts underscore traumatic content, rather than simply describing it.
The spelling âMariiâ is inconsistent with standard usage; correct to âMarjiâ throughout to maintain clarity.
Incorporate additional scholarly sources beyond the two listed to deepen your textual support and strengthen your credibility.
Your discussion of speech censorship asserts its effects but would benefit from one more concrete textual example to solidify your point.
The conclusion neatly summarizes key points and returns to your thesis, reinforcing your essayâs structure.
Elevate your final paragraph by reflecting on Satrapiâs broader cultural critique or the graphic novelâs impact on contemporary readers.
Works Cited entry for Afary lacks publisher and access date; follow full MLA formatting to ensure accuracy.
Labeling âWorks Citedâ clearly signals scholarly rigor; your academic tone is reinforced by consistent section headers.
Add page numbers to footers or headers on each page to enhance navigation and professional presentation.
The sentence summarizing the previous paragraph adds repetition without new insight; consider trimming or repurposing it to maintain analytical flow.
